imagemagick_path: Path to magick.exe or on linux/macOS just /usr/bin/convert

llm_details.NN_llm_provider_comments: Only supported providers are Ollama, LM Studio, and OpenRouter.
llm_details.NN_llm_base_url_comments: Any endpoint - ollama or any openAI compatible endpoint
llm_details.NN_llm_api_key_comments: Only needed for providers that require an API key, like OpenRouter.

llm_image_details.NN_llm_image_provider_comments: Only supported provider for image generation is ComfyUI at the moment
llm_image_details.NN_image_model_comments: This should be the model name as it appears in ComfyUI, including any subdirectories. For example, if your model is located in a subdirectory called 'stablediffusion' and the model file is named 'stablediffusion-2.1.safetensors', you would enter 'stablediffusion/stablediffusion-2.1'. Make sure to use double backslashes (\\) or single forward slashes (/) as path separators, depending on your operating system.
llm_image_details.NN_image_aspect_ratio_comments: This aspect ratio will be included in the prompt for image generation. For example, if you set it to '16:9', the prompt will include 'in a 16:9 aspect ratio'. This can help guide the image generation model to produce images with the desired dimensions.

tts_details.NN_tts_device_comments: TTS device selection: auto, cpu, or cuda. On Windows, auto defaults to CPU for stability; set cuda to force GPU.
tts_details.NN_tts_voice_file_comments: This should be the path to a .wav file that Chatterbox can use as a voice. The file should be a recording of the voice you want to use for TTS. Make sure the audio is clear and of good quality for the best results. You can use any .wav file, but it's recommended to use a recording of the voice you want to synthesize for better results.
